title: Define Application Shell Properties
text: Define Application Shell Properties This editor is displayed to define the properties and triggers (if applicable) of an application shell. The type of application shell, as specified by the Shell Type property, determines the properties and triggers that you can define in this editor. For more information, see Application Shells . Tip: The Syntax hint provides information on the syntax or other information for the selected property. Use the More tab to define your own properties. Properties typically have both a logical names used for display and a technical name, which may be used to address the property in ProcScript (using $applproperties ) or configuration files ( usys.ini ). You can display this technical name by selecting Show technical names . How to Define a Windows Application Shell Define a Character Mode Application Shell Define an Application Server Shell Common Properties Shell Type Description Comments Properties and Triggers If there are many properties specific to a type, they are displayed in different tabs. Windows and Character Mode Properties Triggers Title (Windows only) Library Menu Bar Pop-up Menu Panel Session Panel Panel Panel Position trigger menu pulldown trigger keyboardSwitch trigger userKey For Windows applications, you can set addition properties to control the appearance of a background image (optional) and application window in the Image and Other tabs. For more information, see Properties of Windows Application Shells . For character-based applications, you can define the layout of the application shell. Server Properties Triggers Library trigger apStart trigger receiveMessage trigger preRequest trigger postRequest For more information, see Application Shells for Servers . Related Links Executing Applications You can execute the application using uniface.exe or make a copy of uniface.exe with your application name. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
